evidence_missing: company official disclosure or press release
pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: OBLG exhibits a sharp 5-day decline of -13.36% and 20-day decline of -40.56% with elevated relative volume (2.605x 20d average). However, the evidence packet reveals a STRUCTURAL ABSENCE pattern consistent with a micro-cap, shell entity, or delisted ticker: (1) SEC adapters fail (cik_not_found for sec_edgar, sec_form4); (2) zero news items within cutoff window; (3) fundamental snapshot is insufficient_evidence with all quarterly metrics null; (4) GitHub unmapped; (5) analyst coverage stale (last update 2024-11-01, 1 Buy rating); (6) company has undergone multiple reverse splits (1:40, 1:15, 1:10 historically), signaling distressed capital structure; (7) all recent earnings are deeply negative (EPS -0.7721, -11.25, -1.6, -2.8); (8) valuation metrics are negative (PE -0.94, net margin -260.77%, ROE -0.81). The narrative_event agent classified the catalyst as VAGUE (unknown_or_absent) due to absence of news, SEC filings, and fundamental data. The alt_data_verify agent returned insufficient_evidence_to_verify with expected_blocks_absent=[sec_edgar, sec_form4, sec_13f, github_public, news_event_summary, fundamental_snapshot]. The surge_short agent failed schema validation and returned needs_more_evidence. Risk agent confirmed no hard-veto conditions tripped. Per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception, the structural absence of corroborating evidence (T1/T2 filings, news, GitHub, insider data) is itself bear-thesis evidence for a surge_short candidate—the public narrative (if any) lacks hard-to-fake corroboration, which is the pump signal that motivates the short. The price decline is real and substantial; the absence of identifiable catalyst combined with negative fundamentals and distressed capital structure (reverse splits) suggests either a genuine distressed equity or a pump-and-dump pattern. The macro regime is Strengthening/Expansionary with strict equity discipline, which argues against quality-long entry but does not preclude surge-short on a bearish technical + narrative-divergence setup. The preponderance of evidence—negative earnings, reverse splits, structural absence of SEC/news/GitHub corroboration, vague narrative, elevated volume on decline—supports a bear thesis. Position sizing per §4.8 is fixed at 0.005 (0.5%) for new surge_short entries. No hard-veto conditions tripped. Decision: SHORT at 0.5% entry.
invalidation_conditions: If OBLG is a legitimate operating company and the SEC adapter failures are data quality issues (not genuine CIK absence), the bear thesis weakens.
If a catalyst exists in the filtered news set (20 items dropped post-cutoff) or in OTC Markets disclosures outside the adapter window, the vague narrative classification would be incomplete.
If the reverse splits are historical (pre-2023) and the company has since stabilized operationally, the distressed-capital-structure signal may be stale.
If analyst coverage has resumed post-2024-11-01 with positive updates, the sentiment picture may have shifted.
If the price decline is driven by sector-wide or macro contagion rather than company-specific distress, the bear thesis is less company-specific.

pm_decision_extract.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure assumes that a catalyst will be present in news_event_summary or filing_confirmation. When both are absent or unavailable (as in OBLG), the agent has no primary narrative to classify. The schema does not provide a dedicated field for 'no catalyst identified' or 'catalyst unknown'; the agent must infer this from null/empty blocks. Additionally, the packet does not distinguish between 'adapter failed' (cik_not_found) and 'ticker is genuinely inactive/delisted', making it difficult to assess whether the absence of data is informative or a data quality issue. A pre-flight check for ticker validity (listing status, CIK existence) would improve clarity. For surge_short candidates, the structural absence of corroborating evidence is itself bear-thesis input per §5.17, but the schema's fail-closed posture on information_integrity_assessment.use_as_primary_signal_allowed limits explicit elevation of this signal.
recommended_followups: Verify OBLG's current listing status on NASDAQ and SEC EDGAR.
If OBLG is delisted or inactive, flag the ticker as ineligible for analysis.
If OBLG is active, investigate why SEC adapters fail and manually retrieve CIK.
Search for OBLG press releases or investor relations announcements outside SEC channels.
Check OTC Markets and Pink Sheets for OBLG trading activity and disclosures.
If a catalyst is found in the filtered news set (post-cutoff), re-run analysis with expanded cutoff window.
Assess whether the price decline is driven by a sector-wide or macro event rather than company-specific news.
Monitor OBLG for cover triggers: price rebound >50%, positive catalyst emergence, or days_held threshold per §10.14.

narrative_event.reasoning_summary: OBLG exhibits a sharp 5-day decline of -13.36% and 20-day decline of -40.56% with elevated relative volume (2.605x 20d average). However, the evidence packet contains NO identifiable catalyst. news_event_summary.status='data_unavailable' with 0 items within the cutoff window and 20 items dropped post-cutoff, indicating the news adapter returned no contemporaneous events. filing_confirmation.status='not_evaluated' with sec_edgar adapter failure (cik_not_found), preventing SEC filing verification. fundamental_snapshot.status='insufficient_evidence' with all quarterly metrics null. The only alternative data present is wikipedia_pageviews for 'Atlantean_language' (an unrelated article), which shows modest fluctuation (55-120 views/day) with no spike pattern correlating to the price decline. Analyst sentiment from fmp_sentiment shows 1 Buy rating (as of 2024-11-01) with no recent changes. The corporate calendar shows 4 past earnings events (all negative EPS: -0.7721, -11.25, -1.6, -2.8) and 2 stock splits (1:40 on 2024-08-26, 1:15 on 2023-01-04), but no upcoming events within the decision window. The price action appears disconnected from any disclosed catalyst. The structural absence of news, SEC filings, and fundamental data—combined with the company's inability to be mapped to SEC CIK or GitHub—suggests either: (1) a micro-cap or shell entity with minimal disclosure obligations, (2) a delisted or inactive ticker, or (3) a data integrity issue. Without a concrete, named catalyst (specific customer, contract, regulatory event, or earnings surprise), the narrative is VAGUE by default. The price decline is real, but its cause is not identifiable from the evidence packet.

If OBLG is a shell company or delisted ticker, the absence of data is expected and not indicative of a hidden catalyst.

If the price decline is driven by sector-wide or macro contagion rather than company-specific news, the lack of a named catalyst is consistent with that pattern.

alt_data_verify.decision_or_assessment: Alternative-data verification of OBLG narrative against available evidence
alt_data_verify.reasoning_summary: OBLG presents a structural absence of corroborating evidence across multiple critical alt-data channels. The company cannot be matched to SEC filings (cik_not_found), has no GitHub presence (unmapped_ticker), and returned zero news items in the 7-day window. The only alt-data signal present is Wikipedia pageviews for 'Atlantean_language' (30 rows, 2025-03-24 to 2025-04-22), which shows modest daily traffic (55–120 views) with no clear spike pattern relative to the decision date. FMP sentiment data (T2 tier, 40 rows) shows 1 analyst buy rating as of 2024-11-01, with no recent updates. Price action shows -13.36% return over 5 days and -40.56% over 20 days, with elevated relative volume (2.605x 20-day average). The macro regime is 'Strengthening/Expansionary' with strict equity discipline. Fundamental snapshot is insufficient_evidence (no PIT-safe quarterly data). The information_integrity_assessment flags low-tier-only evidence (T3=1, T4=1) with use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false per schema v1.1 fail-closed policy. No coordinated campaign risk detected, but pollution_risk_level is 'unknown'. The absence of SEC filings, insider activity, and news coverage—combined with the company's inability to be mapped to standard financial databases—suggests either a micro-cap, delisted, or shell entity. This structural absence relative to what a legitimate operating company should produce is itself bear-thesis evidence for a surge_short candidate.
invalidation_conditions: If OBLG is a legitimate operating company, the absence of SEC CIK and GitHub mapping is anomalous and suggests data quality issues rather than a real business.
If the Wikipedia pageviews are unrelated to the ticker (e.g., a data-mapping error), the only alt-data signal evaporates.
If a recent 8-K or press release exists but was dropped post-cutoff, the narrative gap may be explained by a catalyst not yet visible in the packet.
If analyst coverage has resumed post-2024-11-01, the sentiment picture may have shifted.

This prevents elevation of low-tier evidence even when structural absence is itself informative. For surge_short candidates, the absence pattern (cik_not_found, unmapped_ticker, zero news) is bear-thesis evidence, but the schema's fail-closed posture on primary signals limits explicit corroboration. The Wikipedia pageviews adapter is a fallback default and may not be relevant to OBLG's actual business.

Manually verify SEC EDGAR for any filings under alternative CIK or name.
Check OTC Markets or Pink Sheets for OBLG if it has moved off NASDAQ.
Obtain analyst reports or institutional ownership data from alternative sources (e.g., FactSet, Bloomberg).
Review corporate actions (splits, reverse splits) to understand historical capital structure.
If OBLG is a legitimate micro-cap, consider whether the absence of coverage is itself a signal of low institutional interest.

risk.reasoning_summary: Pass 8 hard-veto evaluation for OBLG. Candidate type not specified in input; evaluating veto conditions mechanically per §5.13. Condition (a): equity cap breach — not applicable without candidate_type=quality_long and proposed position size. Condition (b): short sleeve cap breach — not applicable without candidate_type=surge_short and proposed position size. Condition (c): critical data_quality_flags — examined all flags; highest severity is 'warning' (quote_after_cutoff, alt_data_adapter_error, fundamentals_partial, news_post_cutoff_filtered); no 'critical' severity flags present. Condition (d): anti-pollution integrity veto — information_integrity_assessment.pollution_confirmed is not set to true (value is unimplemented per schema v1.1 decision #8); pollution_defense_layer status is 'insufficient_evidence' with use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false due to fail-closed default, NOT due to affirmatively-confirmed pollution. Per §5.13(d) relaxation 2026-05-06, when pollution_defense_layer returns status ∈ {not_evaluated, not_connected, pending, unimplemented}, §5.13(d) does NOT trip. Condition (e): lookahead veto — envelope.data_after_cutoff_used=false and envelope.lookahead_safe=true; no lookahead violation.
invalidation_conditions: If candidate_type is later specified as quality_long and proposed position would breach §4.7 equity cap, condition (a) would trip.
If candidate_type is later specified as surge_short and proposed position would breach §4.8 sleeve cap (>10%), condition (b) would trip.
If any data_quality_flags[*].severity is upgraded to 'critical' post-evaluation, condition (c) would trip.

If information_integrity_assessment.pollution_confirmed is affirmatively set to true (not unimplemented/not_evaluated/pending) AND use_as_primary_signal_allowed=false AND no T1/T2 corroboration, condition (d) would trip for quality_long candidates only.
If envelope.data_after_cutoff_used is changed to true or envelope.lookahead_safe is changed to false, condition (e) would trip.

risk.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: Pass 8 hard-veto evaluation is mechanical and does not require candidate_type specification. However, conditions (a) and (b) are position-sizing vetoes that require candidate_type and proposed position size to evaluate. The input packet does not include proposed position size or candidate_type; therefore, conditions (a) and (b) cannot be tripped by this evaluation. Condition (d) anti-pollution veto is relaxed per §5.13(d) 2026-05-06 to NOT trip when pollution_defense_layer is unimplemented/not_evaluated/pending; this preserves fail-closed semantics on affirmatively-confirmed pollution while restoring evaluability when the defense layer is not yet implemented. The current packet has pollution_defense_layer status='insufficient_evidence' (unimplemented), so condition (d) does not trip.
recommended_followups: Provide candidate_type (surge_short or quality_long) and proposed position size to enable full veto evaluation including conditions (a) and (b).
Implement pollution-defense layer to move information_integrity_assessment from fail-closed default to affirmative confirmation.
Verify OBLG's SEC CIK and listing status; if delisted or shell, escalate to risk team.
If OBLG is a legitimate micro-cap, obtain alternative fundamental and news data sources.
Re-run veto evaluation once candidate_type and position size are specified.

pm.reasoning_summary: OBLG exhibits a sharp 5-day decline of -13.36% and 20-day decline of -40.56% with elevated relative volume (2.605x 20d average). However, the evidence packet reveals a STRUCTURAL ABSENCE pattern consistent with a micro-cap, shell entity, or delisted ticker: (1) SEC adapters fail (cik_not_found for sec_edgar, sec_form4); (2) zero news items within cutoff window; (3) fundamental snapshot is insufficient_evidence with all quarterly metrics null; (4) GitHub unmapped; (5) analyst coverage stale (last update 2024-11-01, 1 Buy rating); (6) company has undergone multiple reverse splits (1:40, 1:15, 1:10 historically), signaling distressed capital structure; (7) all recent earnings are deeply negative (EPS -0.7721, -11.25, -1.6, -2.8); (8) valuation metrics are negative (PE -0.94, net margin -260.77%, ROE -0.81). The narrative_event agent classified the catalyst as VAGUE (unknown_or_absent) due to absence of news, SEC filings, and fundamental data. The alt_data_verify agent returned insufficient_evidence_to_verify with expected_blocks_absent=[sec_edgar, sec_form4, sec_13f, github_public, news_event_summary, fundamental_snapshot]. The surge_short agent failed schema validation and returned needs_more_evidence. Risk agent confirmed no hard-veto conditions tripped. Per §5.17 surge-short integrity-veto exception, the structural absence of corroborating evidence (T1/T2 filings, news, GitHub, insider data) is itself bear-thesis evidence for a surge_short candidate—the public narrative (if any) lacks hard-to-fake corroboration, which is the pump signal that motivates the short. The price decline is real and substantial; the absence of identifiable catalyst combined with negative fundamentals and distressed capital structure (reverse splits) suggests either a genuine distressed equity or a pump-and-dump pattern. The macro regime is Strengthening/Expansionary with strict equity discipline, which argues against quality-long entry but does not preclude surge-short on a bearish technical + narrative-divergence setup. The preponderance of evidence—negative earnings, reverse splits, structural absence of SEC/news/GitHub corroboration, vague narrative, elevated volume on decline—supports a bear thesis. Position sizing per §4.8 is fixed at 0.005 (0.5%) for new surge_short entries. No hard-veto conditions tripped. Decision: SHORT at 0.5% entry.
invalidation_conditions: If OBLG is a legitimate operating company and the SEC adapter failures are data quality issues (not genuine CIK absence), the bear thesis weakens.
If a catalyst exists in the filtered news set (20 items dropped post-cutoff) or in OTC Markets disclosures outside the adapter window, the vague narrative classification would be incomplete.
If the reverse splits are historical (pre-2023) and the company has since stabilized operationally, the distressed-capital-structure signal may be stale.
If analyst coverage has resumed post-2024-11-01 with positive updates, the sentiment picture may have shifted.
If the price decline is driven by sector-wide or macro contagion rather than company-specific distress, the bear thesis is less company-specific.

pm.schema_or_prompt_weakness_notes: The evidence packet structure assumes that a catalyst will be present in news_event_summary or filing_confirmation. When both are absent or unavailable (as in OBLG), the agent has no primary narrative to classify. The schema does not provide a dedicated field for 'no catalyst identified' or 'catalyst unknown'; the agent must infer this from null/empty blocks. Additionally, the packet does not distinguish between 'adapter failed' (cik_not_found) and 'ticker is genuinely inactive/delisted', making it difficult to assess whether the absence of data is informative or a data quality issue. A pre-flight check for ticker validity (listing status, CIK existence) would improve clarity. For surge_short candidates, the structural absence of corroborating evidence is itself bear-thesis input per §5.17, but the schema's fail-closed posture on information_integrity_assessment.use_as_primary_signal_allowed limits explicit elevation of this signal.
recommended_followups: Verify OBLG's current listing status on NASDAQ and SEC EDGAR.
If OBLG is delisted or inactive, flag the ticker as ineligible for analysis.
If OBLG is active, investigate why SEC adapters fail and manually retrieve CIK.
Search for OBLG press releases or investor relations announcements outside SEC channels.
Check OTC Markets and Pink Sheets for OBLG trading activity and disclosures.
If a catalyst is found in the filtered news set (post-cutoff), re-run analysis with expanded cutoff window.
Assess whether the price decline is driven by a sector-wide or macro event rather than company-specific news.
Monitor OBLG for cover triggers: price rebound >50%, positive catalyst emergence, or days_held threshold per §10.14.
pm.reason: OBLG exhibits -40.56% 20-day decline with elevated volume (2.605x 20d avg) and structural absence of SEC filings, news, and fundamental data. Negative earnings (-0.77 to -11.25 EPS), multiple reverse splits, and vague narrative (no identifiable catalyst) support bear thesis. Absence of hard-to-fake corroboration (T1/T2 filings, GitHub, insider data) is itself pump signal per §5.17. Entry at 0.005 (0.5%) per §4.8 fixed sizing. No hard-veto conditions tripped. High uncertainty due to data gaps; monitor for cover triggers.
